name: 67-classify-ip-row-failclose
_comment: Revert of the unsound P2 `ip`-row drop. The classify {age} axis has rows keyed on @testverify (an `ip` matcher resolving the REAL client IP). The worker DOES see that IP for a public visitor, so dropping the row would diverge the edge from the server (it would compute a different {age} and cache a variant the server never produces). Instead the `ip` row fail-closes the whole classifier, so a cache_key reading {age} fail-opens: the edge IR delegates with recv.pass=[{always:true}] + a `directive: ip` delegate entry (see the generated .ir.json), while the Cadish server behind evaluates `ip` and computes the correct variant. This is a DELEGATED/fail-open site: the edge (pass=true) and the server (pass=false, real {age} key) intentionally diverge, so — like 68 — it carries NO request cases (a Go==JS golden is impossible by design); the IR golden alone proves the fail-close + delegate. No row is dropped; no divergent edge cache.
